symptoms.symptom_name: Scintillating scotoma — jagged, flickering, partly blind patch in the right visual field, resolved

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A scintillating scotoma that evolves and resolves is a hallmark visual aura.
Transient monocular/hemifield visual loss can be vascular; on a first event it must be actively excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right-hand paraesthesia that marched up the arm and resolved

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A slow sensory march over minutes is typical of cortical spreading depression rather than a sudden vascular deficit.
Unilateral limb numbness is a stroke/TIA symptom until excluded by examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual onset and spread (march) of aura symptoms over minutes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gradual build-up and spread over minutes favours migrainous aura.
TIA deficits are typically maximal at onset rather than marching — helpful but not definitive on a first presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe unilateral throbbing headache with photophobia following the aura

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A unilateral throbbing headache with photophobia after an aura completes the migraine picture.
Unilateral throbbing pain with photophobia and a preceding aura is not tension-type.

symptoms.symptom_name: Currently taking a combined (oestrogen-containing) oral contraceptive

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migraine with aura combined with exogenous oestrogen markedly increases ischaemic-stroke risk; oestrogen is an absolute contraindication in this setting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no neck stiffness, no rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, neck stiffness and rash makes meningitis unlikely.
A build-up rather than an instantaneous peak lowers concern for SAH, but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal focal neurological examination — cranial nerves intact, power and sensation 5/5 and symmetrical, full visual fields, no cerebellar signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal inter-ictal exam reassures against an active deficit — but it is a sealed clinician finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain, which is exactly why in-person assessment is required.
Absence of bruits is neither sensitive nor specific; a clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured normal blood pressure and heart rate (BP 125/75, HR 85), afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals are reassuring but do not exclude a cerebrovascular cause; a sealed clinician measurement, and also the BP/BMI needed before prescribing an alternative pill.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neuroimaging (CT/MRI brain) to exclude an acute cerebrovascular event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging may be needed on a first focal neuro event with atypical or persistent features; not available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The gradual, spreading, resolving visual then sensory symptoms followed by a unilateral throbbing headache with photophobia together define migraine with aura clinically.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Ischaemic stroke risk (contraindication to combined hormonal contraception)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Aura plus a combined oestrogen pill is the specific combination that makes the oestrogen an absolute contraindication and mandates immediate cessation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A first focal neurological event must be examined in person to exclude a cerebrovascular cause — the primary reason the AI Doctor cannot clear this remotely.
Required baseline before a clinician prescribes an alternative (non-oestrogen) contraceptive.
Considered by the in-person clinician if features are atypical, persistent or the diagnosis is uncertain; not routinely required for a resolved classic aura with a normal exam.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate empirical thrombolysis or aspirin loading on history alone
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No stroke treatment should be initiated on remote history without examination/imaging; the correct action is in-person assessment, not over-investigation or empirical stroke therapy.
